title: lotoxalis sepium
intro: Lotoxalis sepium, commonly known as the yellow wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). The plant's leaves and stems contain oxalic acid, which has been used for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Lotoxalis sepium include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate rheumatic pains, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
